Meet with Tromml’s CEO

Investor Memo · June 2026 · Limited Angel Round

Turning everyday sales activity into business intelligence

The aftermarket runs on relationships, and the knowledge behind them lives in reps’ heads, where the business can’t use it. Tromml captures it and makes it usable across the organization. Minecart, our activity intelligence platform for industrial distribution, turns the field context reps never log into the next best action, so managers finally see what’s happening in their accounts. What makes this ours to win: in under three years we’ve become the most recognized AI voice in the aftermarket, with direct access to industry leaders outsiders will never reach. In beta, reps captured 10x the context they log today, surfacing millions in opportunities their teams couldn’t see.

The problem

Anyone who has run a business knows the best information lives in people's heads. In a relationship-driven industry, it is even more true. Most of the real work happens in the field, on the phone, and at the counter, far from any office, with no good way to capture it.

The little that does get written down just sits in a system. CRMs were built to store records, not to act on them. So the frontline team burns time on call reports nobody reads, managers are left guessing about what is really happening in their accounts, and the business never builds anything it can grow on. Every night, hard-won knowledge walks out the door.

Our bet

Most enterprise AI is failing, and the models are not the problem. The knowledge that runs a business was never captured, so there is nothing real for AI to act on. That gap between what people know and what the business can use is the true ceiling on how far a company can scale.

We believe the next workforce is agentic, and it will be built one industry at a time. That takes more than a generic vendor. It takes a partner the industry trusts to understand how the work actually gets done. Adoption was never about the technology. It is about the daily reality of how people work, and having someone beside them who understands it.

Whoever captures the front line first owns the context everything else needs, and that context is the moat. We build it as a loop that compounds, where every field interaction makes the next one smarter.

The solution

Minecart is the capture layer. A rep taps one button and talks, and every field visit, phone call, and piece of sales data becomes a clean, structured record. No call reports, no data entry, nothing to remember later.

Then Tromml refines it. The name comes from the trommel, the rotating screen miners use to separate valuable ore from waste. We do the same with field data: cut the noise, surface the risks, opportunities, and patterns that matter, and route each one to the person who can act on it.

It is effortless for reps: one tap instead of the call reports they dread, so they actually use it. Managers get a live pulse on performance and the issues bubbling up before they cost money. And because it all becomes structured intelligence, the same field signal feeds marketing, purchasing, and leadership, not just sales.

Here is how that intelligence moves through the business.

In practice: a rep at Tri-City Auto Care notes a brake that keeps coming back. For the rep, that is one tap, no report to write, and a follow-up that will not slip. By Friday the same flag has surfaced at six more shops, so the manager sees a pattern, not a one-off, and acts early. Because it is captured once and shared, the issue routes back to the manufacturer and warns purchasing, all before a quiet defect costs millions. One conversation, value for the rep, the manager, and the whole business.

Proof

We are early, and that is the point. With only a handful of tests, the signal is already loud.

In our beta, field reps captured 10x more context than they log today, and that activity surfaced millions of dollars in opportunities their teams could not see before. When one global manufacturer ran Tromml at two trade shows, twelve reps logged 182 notes where the year before there were zero, giving their president his first real read on trade-show ROI.

Tromml has already surfaced tens of thousands of dollars in opportunities we would have otherwise missed, and it lets me strategize better with my reps.

Who we are

Tromml is led by CEO Lauren McCullough and co-founder Harry Park. We are not outsiders who learned the aftermarket from a slide deck. We live in it.

In a relationship business, trust is not marketing. It is distribution. We keynote the industry's biggest stages, including AAPEX, SEMA, Auto Care, and MEMA, and we are regularly invited and paid to run AI workshops. We do not just show up. We teach.

That has opened doors most software never reaches: real conversations with some of the top CEOs and companies in the country, and the relationships that brought on leading distributors and suppliers like NexaMotion Group, Auto-Wares, and SKF.

The opportunity

The aftermarket runs on relationships, and still runs on phone calls and spreadsheets. Across North America, the markets our customers serve top half a trillion dollars in light-duty automotive, commercial vehicle, and powersports. Three big markets, same relationships, same broken tools, same blind spot. Nobody can see their own field activity. And it is a reference point, not a ceiling: we can serve other regions, and we support multiple steps of the supply chain, from suppliers to distributors to the field.

It's a huge niche almost no one is building for. Not glamorous, which is why it's wide open. The people moving billions in parts have no software that understands how they work. We do.

And the markets connect. The same reps and distributors span automotive, commercial vehicle, and powersports, so each win sets up the next. Beyond that, any relationship-heavy distribution industry with the same problem: heavy equipment, industrial supply, building products.

And it gets bigger. The shift coming is agentic. An agent that does the work captures the value of the work, not just a software fee, and agents are only as good as the context they run on. That context does not exist in this industry yet. It lives in conversations and field judgment no system has captured. Minecart is building it. Whoever owns the context owns the agents, and we intend to own the context.

The pull is already global, with work underway in Europe and inbound interest from North Africa.

How we make money

Tromml is software with a land-and-expand model.

  • Land. Reps and managers pay a recurring per-seat fee for the app that captures field activity and gives leadership visibility. It is low friction and fast to adopt, which makes it an easy first yes.
  • Expand. As more of the team captures more context, that intelligence opens higher-value work across the company: manager reporting, forecasting, market intelligence, purchasing signals, and AI decision support. Revenue per account grows as we earn more of the organization.
  • Compound. The operational context we accumulate is proprietary and gets richer every day. That is what makes the later automation and agents possible, and very hard for anyone else to copy.

Training is part of how we land, not a side business. We get paid to run AI sessions at industry events, which covers much of our travel and business development and turns those rooms into customers.

What we are raising for

We are raising $150K to support the new enterprise pilots we are bringing on and keep our focus on sales, so we do not have to ease off the accelerator at exactly the moment it is working. We are gearing up for a larger, priced raise in the fall.

Investors coming in now get a strong deal, joining on our existing SAFE ahead of that round. We are not asking you to fund the risky part. That work is done. With real revenue, live deployments, and the industry's trust already in hand, this is about as derisked as early investing gets. We are backed by Techstars, NC IDEA Seed, Tweener Fund and Scot Wingo (founder of ChannelAdvisor), Greg Kerber (founder of PartsTech), and Lisa Coyle (Payments360), and we would like you in the story too.

Built for the aftermarket, Tromml turns complex sales data into clear insights that drive growth and efficiency.
